learnings.subject: Emergency Dematter Cream
learnings.learning: Dematter paid ROAS fell to RED (1.43 vs 2.07 breakeven) in the same window its 'detangler for dogs' organic rank slid 8->13 - possible pattern: rising paid spend masking organic decline; check organic rank before scaling Dematter paid.
evidence: ROAS 1.43 on $318.18 spend (Supermetrics Query B, 2026-07-08)
Organic rank 'detangler for dogs' 8->13 (H10 Daily Keyword Tracker, 2026-07-08)

2026-07-10: 'detangler for dogs' rank slid further to 7 (from a baseline of 5), and 'dog detangler' dropped 9 spots (7->16) - organic instability continues, but paid ROAS remains unverifiable (Supermetrics down 2nd straight day) so this cannot be confirmed or contradicted today

2026-07-11: 'detangler for dogs' rank collapsed 7->23 (-16 spots), the largest single-day drop yet in this pattern (H10 Daily Keyword Tracker, report date 2026-07-10); other Dematter keywords ('dog detangler', 'dematting comb for dogs', 'dematting spray for dogs') all improved the same day, so this is isolated to one keyword, not a whole-listing decline. Paid ROAS impact still unverifiable - Supermetrics 3rd consecutive day of total outage

2026-07-12: 'detangler for dogs' reversed sharply, 23->8 (+15 spots), the largest single-day recovery yet (H10 Daily Keyword Tracker, report date 2026-07-11) - the keyword is now more volatile in both directions than any other Dematter keyword. Paid ROAS impact still unverifiable - Supermetrics 4th consecutive day of total outage. This does not confirm the original 'decline' direction, but does reinforce that this specific keyword is unusually unstable; driver remains unidentified without paid-spend visibility.

2026-07-13: 'detangler for dogs' and 'dog detangler' are absent entirely from today's Daily Keyword Tracker report (only 'dematting tool for dogs' and 'dematting comb for dogs' listed for this SKU) - report display gap, not a confirmed rank change either direction. Paid ROAS impact still unverifiable - Supermetrics 5th consecutive day of total outage.

2026-07-14: both keywords reappeared in the report (H10 Daily Keyword Tracker, report date 2026-07-13) - 'detangler for dogs' rank 12 (-4) and 'dog detangler' rank 13 (-4), both down vs H10's own day-over-day indicator. No Step 0 baseline available since both were absent the prior day. Paid ROAS is now verifiable again (Supermetrics pipeline resolved) - Dematter ROAS 1.83 (YELLOW, not RED) on $300.75 spend/$551.76 attributed sales (Supermetrics Query B, date 2026-07-12), so today's organic dip is NOT coinciding with a paid ROAS collapse - continues to weaken the original 'masking' hypothesis while confirming the keyword's volatility.

2026-07-16 (data Jul 14-15): H10 Keyword Tracker (report date 2026-07-15) shows 'dog detangler' at organic rank 17 (-5 spots, SV 2,052), 'detangler for dogs' rank 14 (+4, SV 1,561), 'dematting spray for dogs' rank 3 (-1, SV 1,829), 'dematting comb for dogs' rank 22 (+4, SV 5,818). Mixed signals: 'dog detangler' continues its downward drift (-5) while 'detangler for dogs' recovered (+4). Paid ROAS on Jul 14: 1.993 (YELLOW, Supermetrics Query B) — still not in collapse alongside organic drift. Pattern remains inconclusive.

2026-07-17 (data Jul 15-16): H10 Keyword Tracker (report date 2026-07-16) shows 'detangler for dogs' at rank 17 (-3 spots from 14, SV 1,561) — 3-spot drop threshold triggered. 'dematting comb for dogs' at rank 26 (-4 spots from 22, SV 5,818) — 4-spot drop threshold triggered. 'dematting spray for dogs' stable at rank 3. 'dog detangler' rank 19 (-2). Additionally, Dematter paid ROAS on Jul 15: 1.578 (RED band, band change from YELLOW on Jul 14 — Supermetrics Query B, spend $157.35, attributed sales $248.29). Two Dematter organic keywords in simultaneous decline alongside paid ROAS dropping into RED — strengthens the original hypothesis vs. prior contradictions.

2026-07-18 (data Jul 16-17): H10 Keyword Tracker (report date 2026-07-17) shows Emergency Dematter recovering — two keywords improved +2 spots each ('detangler for dogs' ~rank 15, 'dematting comb for dogs' ~rank 24), reversing the prior day's simultaneous -3 and -4 drops. No new 3+ spot drops for any Dematter keyword. Paid ROAS on Jul 16: 1.589 (RED band, 2nd consecutive RED day — Supermetrics Query B, spend $156.23, attributed sales $248.29). Pattern: organic keywords bounced back while paid ROAS remains in RED for a 2nd day. The organic recovery weakens the co-occurrence evidence from Jul 16 (the only day organic + paid declined together); prior volatility history (e.g., 23→8 reversal on Jul 12) shows one-day reversals are characteristic of these keywords. Masking hypothesis remains open but requires 3+ consecutive days of both organic decline AND paid RED/KILL to be VALIDATED.
learnings.action_implication: Before any Dematter budget increase, verify organic ranks are stable; if organic is sliding, fix the listing before adding spend. Organic recovered today (+2 on two keywords) after yesterday's simultaneous drops — the masking hypothesis requires sustained co-occurrence of both organic decline AND paid RED/KILL across 3+ days. Current status: paid ROAS RED for 2 consecutive days (Jul 15 and Jul 16), organic mixed (dropped Jul 16, recovered Jul 17). Monitor next 2 data points; if organic drops again alongside continued RED/KILL paid ROAS, the masking hypothesis graduates to VALIDATED. With paid ROAS in RED, review bid caps before scaling any Dematter budget.

learnings.subject: Supermetrics schedule_id pipeline
learnings.learning: Root cause identified 2026-07-13 — TWO stacked failures: (1) Supermetrics schedule_ids expire before morning collection regardless of gap length (both ~8h45m and ~4h gaps failed), so a submit-overnight/redeem-morning handoff can never work; (2) separately, the Amazon Ads data source (ds AA) hangs indefinitely on all report jobs — auth shows AUTHENTICATED and accounts list fine, but jobs never complete (43+ min), while Amazon SC (ASELL) and Google Ads (AW) complete normally. Fix (1) shipped: Evening Pre-Run v5 polls queries to completion at 11 PM and stores RESULTS in data/supermetrics_data.json; Morning Routine v10.6 reads the file, never redeems IDs. Fix (2) pending: Amazon Ads connection re-auth by Will at hub.supermetrics.com/token-management (connected via his Amazon login).
evidence: supermetrics_ids.json submitted_at: 2026-07-08 23:04:59 PT
All 6 schedule_ids (A-F) returned RuntimeError 'schedule_id has expired' on first poll, 2026-07-09 morning run

2026-07-10: same failure repeated - schedule_ids submitted 2026-07-09 23:05:55 PT, all 6 (A-F) again returned 'schedule_id has expired' on first poll at the 2026-07-10 morning run (~8h45m gap again). 2nd consecutive day of total (6-for-6) outage - confirms this is a systemic gap-length issue, not a one-off.

2026-07-11: 3rd consecutive day of total (6-for-6) outage - schedule_ids submitted 2026-07-10 23:06:57 PT, all 6 (A-F) again returned 'schedule_id has expired' on first poll. Pipeline has now produced zero usable Amazon Ads/Amazon SC/Google Ads data for 3 straight mornings.

2026-07-12: 4th consecutive day of total (6-for-6) outage - schedule_ids submitted 2026-07-12 03:34:10 PT (a much shorter ~4h gap than prior days), all 6 (A-F) still returned 'schedule_id has expired' on first poll. A shorter submission-to-collection gap did NOT fix it this time, which weakens the pure 'gap-length' theory and suggests the retention window may be shorter than assumed, or another systemic issue is at play.

2026-07-13: 5th consecutive day of total (6-for-6) outage - schedule_ids submitted 2026-07-12 23:06:22 PT (~8h44m gap, back to the longer pattern), all 6 (A-F) again returned 'schedule_id has expired' on first poll.

2026-07-13 (diagnosis): schedule_ids proven to be content-hashes of query parameters — identical IDs day-over-day for relative-date queries is EXPECTED, not evidence of failed resubmission; the old 'different hash = fix confirmed' check could never signal anything.

2026-07-13 (v5 first run): Evening Pre-Run v5 polled to completion — D (Amazon SC sales), E (SC sessions), F (Google Ads) completed and pushed real data to supermetrics_data.json; A/B/C (all ds AA) hung 43 min and never completed. Fresh-hash AA test job (custom dates, unique schedule_id) also hung — rules out stuck-job pinning; the AA data source itself is broken.

2026-07-13 (connection check): data_source_discovery ds AA returned authentication_status AUTHENTICATED and accounts_discovery listed all 4 marketplaces ('Connected via: Will Dryden') — token valid at metadata level while report generation fails silently. Supermetrics trace IDs if support is needed: fa024804dae1583ed581fa313a30d9db, 56214a5ea32dfa604bda663bef5521d6.

2026-07-14: for the first time since 7/8, Queries A/B/C (ds AA) all returned status 'completed' with real per-SKU data (account spend $586.72, ROAS 2.085; 15 SKU rows in Query B) - the predicted AA hang did NOT occur. This contradicts the 'AA data source is broken' driver as currently stated; suggests Will's re-auth (or another change) resolved it. One day of success is not yet conclusive.

2026-07-15: Queries A/B/C (ds AA) FAILED again — 'Query stalled: data source reported running but no tasks scheduled'. Confirmed regression from the 2026-07-14 single-day success; the transient fix on 7/14 did not persist. Amazon Ads per-SKU spend/ROAS/TACOS data unavailable; Slicker and unrecognized-ASIN checks NOT RUN this run.

2026-07-16 (morning run): supermetrics_data.json generated by Evening Pre-Run at 2026-07-15 23:07 PT (8.5h prior) was available and FRESH — all 6 queries (A-F) status 'completed' including A/B/C (ds AA). Queries A/B/C returned valid per-SKU data (account spend $394.50, ROAS 1.695, 7 ASIN rows in Query B). The store-results-not-IDs fix (1) is confirmed working; the AA instability pattern appears to have resolved again as of the Jul 15 pre-run. Whether this is durable or another transient fix (as on 7/14) remains to be monitored.

2026-07-17 (morning run): supermetrics_data.json generated by Evening Pre-Run at 2026-07-16 23:04 PT (8.5h prior) available and FRESH — all 6 queries (A-F) status 'completed' including A/B/C (ds AA). Account spend $350.52, ROAS 1.7952, 8 ASIN rows in Query B. Second consecutive successful AA pre-run (Jul 15 and Jul 16 pre-runs both completed). The Jul 15 failure now looks like a 1-day transient regression. AA stability pattern: success Jul 14, fail Jul 15, success Jul 16, success Jul 16 again — net improving. Monitor 2 more pre-runs for 4-day durable streak before upgrading to VALIDATED.

2026-07-18 (morning run): supermetrics_data.json generated by Evening Pre-Run at 2026-07-17 23:06 PT (8.5h prior) available and FRESH — all 6 queries (A-F) status 'completed' including A/B/C (ds AA). Account spend $340.83, ROAS 1.7982, 9 ASIN rows in Query B (7 spending ASINs). Third consecutive successful AA pre-run (Jul 15, Jul 16, Jul 17 pre-runs all completed). AA stability streak: success Jul 14, fail Jul 15, success Jul 16, Jul 17, Jul 18 — three consecutive days of success (post the single failure on Jul 15). One more successful pre-run (Jul 18 tonight) would complete the 4-day streak required for VALIDATED upgrade. The store-results-not-IDs architectural fix continues working as designed.
learnings.action_implication: ARCHITECTURAL RULE (survives this incident): never build a submit-now/collect-later handoff on Supermetrics schedule_ids — poll to completion in the same session and persist DATA, not IDs. DIAGNOSTIC RULE: if ds AA jobs hang >40 min while ASELL/AW complete normally, suspect the Amazon Ads connection in Supermetrics (re-auth at hub.supermetrics.com/token-management, must be the connecting user), not 'Amazon slowness' — AUTHENTICATED status does not prove report jobs work. UPDATE 2026-07-18: Three consecutive successful AA pre-runs (Jul 15, Jul 16, Jul 17). One more successful pre-run tonight completes the 4-day streak for VALIDATED upgrade. Supermetrics support trace IDs fa024804dae1583ed581fa313a30d9db and 56214a5ea32dfa604bda663bef5521d6 remain available if escalation is needed.

learnings.subject: Detangling Treatment / 4-in-1 Shampoo / Pin Brush / Rake Brush
learnings.learning: Low-spend SKUs ($3-$13/day) repeatedly spend with zero attributed sales - sub-$15 daily budgets may be too small to exit the learning phase and convert; trickle spend produces losses with nothing learned.
evidence: 2026-07-08: Detangling $11.51 / Shampoo $3.00 / Pin Brush $4.61, all ROAS 0.00 (Supermetrics Query B, 2026-07-08)

2026-07-14: same pattern confirmed after a 5-day Supermetrics outage, and now extends to a 4th SKU - Detangling Treatment $12.55, Pin Brush $5.05, Rake Brush $4.63, 4-in-1 Shampoo $2.60, all ROAS 0.00/KILL band (Supermetrics Query B, date 2026-07-12). These SKUs are well past the 3-day learning window (flagged since 7/8), so kill-band evaluation is action-eligible, not learning-suppressed.

2026-07-16 (data Jul 14): PARTIAL CONTRADICTION — Detangling Treatment ($11.40 spend) attributed ROAS 1.666 (RED, $18.99 sales) and Pin Brush ($9.45 spend) attributed ROAS 2.115 (YELLOW, $19.99 sales) — both converted on this date despite remaining in sub-$15/day budget range. Rake Brush ($4.06) and Shampoo ($1.10) remain KILL band (ROAS 0). The original 'always-zero' pattern does not hold universally — Detangling and Pin Brush can convert at these spend levels, even if inconsistently.

2026-07-17 (data Jul 15): Detangling Treatment ROAS 3.087 (GREEN band, $12.30 spend, $37.98 attributed sales) — band change RED→GREEN, consecutive conversion day. Pin Brush ROAS 0.00 (KILL band, $7.60 spend) — band change YELLOW→KILL, zero conversion after showing YELLOW the prior day. Rake Brush ($4.85, ROAS 0) and Shampoo ($2.10, ROAS 0) continue consistent KILL-band behavior. Detangling Treatment diverging toward reliable converter; Pin Brush showing high day-to-day ROAS variance; Rake and Shampoo consistent non-converters.

2026-07-18 (data Jul 16): Detangling Treatment ROAS 0.00 (KILL band, $7.80 spend, $0 revenue) — band change GREEN→KILL, reversal after two consecutive GREEN/RED conversion days. Pin Brush ROAS 4.472 (GREEN band, $4.47 spend, $19.99 revenue) — band change KILL→GREEN, a single-day conversion. 4-in-1 Shampoo ROAS 18.173 (GREEN band, $1.10 spend, $19.99 revenue) — band change KILL→GREEN; very low spend converting single unit may be organic-search-assisted, not pure ad conversion. Rake Brush ($3.99 spend, $0 revenue) KILL — consistent 5th data point. All four SKUs continue showing high day-to-day ROAS variance: none has sustained a single band for 3+ consecutive days. The original 'sub-$15 cannot convert' hypothesis is clearly false; the more accurate picture is extreme single-day volatility at these spend levels, making trend-based decisions unreliable without 5+ day windows. Rake Brush remains the only consistent non-converter.
learnings.action_implication: Do NOT act on single-day band changes for these SKUs — all four show extreme day-to-day ROAS variance at sub-$15 spend levels, making any one-day signal unreliable. Evaluate over 5+ day rolling windows only. Rake Brush ($3.99 spend, KILL band 5 consecutive data points, zero conversions) is the only SKU with consistent enough evidence to justify a pause decision. For Detangling Treatment (GREEN two days, KILL today — 3-day window is too volatile to call). For Pin Brush and Shampoo (single GREEN day each) — wait for confirmation. Updated hypothesis: at these spend levels, conversion events are sparse and random enough that daily band classification carries very low signal. Consider either pausing all four until they can be funded above $25/day for a proper test, or evaluating them weekly rather than daily.

learnings.subject: Account tracking hygiene
learnings.learning: ASINs spend in the ad account without existing in roas_bands.json - untracked spend can run unnoticed until the per-SKU pipeline works; every new campaign launch must add its ASIN to roas_bands.json the same day.
evidence: B0GT1H84JM $166.90 spend ROAS 1.41, unrecognized (Supermetrics Query B, 2026-07-08)

2026-07-14: confirmed after the 5-day Supermetrics outage and significantly escalated - B0GT1H84JM now spending $258.62/day (44% of the entire $586.72 account spend, 2nd largest line item after Dematter), still unrecognized. The other two original ASINs (B0GY9WZWKH, B0GXLPJS4C) also still present at $0.84 and $0.21. Additionally 7 MORE unrecognized ASINs appeared that were never seen before 2026-07-14: B0GT5YFVB8 ($0.42 spend), B0GFGDTJ39, B0GT1MFCY5, B0GT1V7V8R, B0GXH3JTK4 (sku STG-WASH-WEAR-BUNDLE-V2), B0GXLTZ495 (sku STG-EASY-BRUSH-DAYS-V1) - all $0 spend today but present in the account - and B0GY9WZWKH's sku is labeled STG-FULL-COAT-CARE-SYSTEM, B0GXLPJS4C's is STG-TANGLE-MAT-BUNDLE-V1. The bundle-style SKU names suggest several new product bundles were launched without being added to tracking (Supermetrics Query B, date 2026-07-12).

2026-07-16 (data Jul 14): RESOLVED — roas_bands.json was updated (by Jul 14) to include all previously unrecognized ASINs including B0GT1H84JM (Save the Shave Bundle), B0GY9WZWKH, B0GXLPJS4C, and all bundle ASINs. Supermetrics Query B today returned 7 ASINs with spend, all recognized in roas_bands.json (zero unrecognized ASIN flags). FurFix (B0FQFP2S9F) is not yet in roas_bands.json but has $0 ad spend so no active tracking gap for ads. The launch-checklist rule (campaign live → add to roas_bands.json same day) should be maintained going forward.
learnings.action_implication: Root cause addressed: roas_bands.json now covers all spending ASINs. Maintain the launch-checklist rule: campaign live → ASIN added to roas_bands.json same day. Remaining gap: B0FQFP2S9F (FurFix) is not in roas_bands.json — add it if/when it starts ad spend. Monitor each run for any new unrecognized ASINs as new products launch.

learnings.subject: Save the Shave UGC Bill (Meta campaign)
learnings.learning: Meta campaign 'Save the Shave UGC Bill _UGC_070306' breached the $75 cumulative-spend / ROAS<1.0 kill threshold on 2026-07-09 ($130.89 spend, ROAS 0.52) and was flagged for a kill/revise decision. As of 2026-07-10 it has continued spending past the threshold ($154.29 MTD, ROAS 0.44) with no kill/revise decision recorded - the flag alone does not stop spend without a human action.
evidence: 2026-07-09: $130.89 7d spend, ROAS 0.52 (Meta MCP, flagged jarvis_metaads_2026-07-09)
2026-07-10: $154.29 MTD spend, ROAS 0.44 - spend increased $23.40 overnight with no revise/kill applied (Meta MCP, account 2158768751536031)

2026-07-11: $178.65 MTD spend (Jul 1-10), ROAS 0.38 - spend increased another $24.36 overnight, ROAS continuing to decline, still no kill/revise decision recorded. 3rd consecutive day flagged with no action taken.

2026-07-12: $196.84 MTD spend (Jul 1-11), ROAS improved slightly to 0.52 on a strong single day (Jul 11: $18.19 spend, $34.18 purchase value, daily ROAS 1.88 - this was the Shopify order reported the same day). Still no kill/revise decision recorded. 4th consecutive day flagged with no action taken, despite one good day within the window.

2026-07-13: $226.34 MTD spend (Jul 1-12), ROAS improved further to 0.84 on another strong single day (Jul 12: $29.50 spend, ~$87 purchase value, daily ROAS 2.96 - matches the 2-order Shopify day reported the same morning). Still no kill/revise decision recorded. 5th consecutive day flagged with no action taken - the campaign is trending toward breakeven (0.44 -> 0.52 -> 0.84) even though cumulative ROAS remains under 1.0.

2026-07-14: $253.27 MTD spend (Jul 1-13), ROAS ticked back down to 0.79 (from 0.84) - yesterday's (7/13) daily spend was $26.76 at a weak 0.37 daily ROAS, breaking the prior two days' strong-day pattern. Still no kill/revise decision recorded. 6th consecutive day flagged with no action taken.

2026-07-15: $284.69 MTD spend (Jul 1-14), ROAS declined further to 0.70 (from 0.79) - 7th consecutive day flagged with no human action taken. ROAS has now dropped for two straight days after the brief Jul 12-13 improvement window, and the cumulative loss from threshold-breach through today is approximately $205 in spend at sub-1.0 ROAS.

2026-07-16 (data MTD): $309.64 MTD spend, ROAS 0.65 — 8th consecutive day with no human action taken. ROAS declined again (0.70 -> 0.65). Cumulative loss from threshold-breach continues to grow; pattern is unambiguous and consistent — the campaign is bleeding spend at KILL-band ROAS with no intervention.

2026-07-17 (data L30D Jun 17-Jul 17): $338.69 L30D spend, ROAS 0.67 — Day 9 with no human action recorded. Slight ROAS uptick (0.65→0.67) does not change KILL-band classification. Cumulative spend above kill threshold now ~$264. A new copy campaign (ID 120249157571500118) shows ACTIVE status at $0 spend — hygiene concern, verify this is intentional and not a duplicate launch. CTR remains 6.05% (hook works per L-2026-07-16-001) — the failure is post-click, not creative.

2026-07-18 (data L30D Jun 18-Jul 17): $355.59 L30D spend, ROAS 0.63 — Day 10 with no human action recorded. ROAS declined (0.67→0.63). Yesterday (Jul 17): $27.19 spend, 419 impressions, 24 clicks, CTR 5.73%. A new paused campaign 'BLZ-20260717-002 Shampoo 50OFF UGC — 2026-07-17' appeared in the account (launched Jul 17, PAUSED, $0 spend) — not an escalation but worth noting as a new campaign launched while the primary campaign remains in kill-breach. Cumulative spend above kill threshold now ~$280. 10 days with no action taken; this is the longest unresolved VALIDATED flag in the learnings file.
learnings.action_implication: VALIDATED (9 confirmations, 0 contradictions, 10 days unresolved): A kill-threshold flag produces no spend reduction without a same-day human decision — the flag alone is not a control mechanism. This campaign has now spent ~$280 above the kill threshold with ROAS in KILL band (0.63 today). This is the longest unresolved VALIDATED issue in the learnings file. Recommend immediate pause or budget-zero on the Meta Save the Shave campaign. A new Shampoo campaign was launched Jul 17 (PAUSED) — does this indicate a creative strategy pivot that also implies pausing the primary? @Will decision needed today. If paused and Dematter creative is ready (L-2026-07-16-002), launch the staged Dematter creative as the replacement test.

learnings.subject: Shopify D2C channel
learnings.learning: Shopify direct-to-consumer sales have been $0 for 4 consecutive days (Jul 7-10), coinciding with $0 email revenue on the only two active Klaviyo flows (both zero-revenue, low-volume). No paid or organic driver in this run's data explains the drought - it may simply reflect very low current Shopify traffic/marketing investment rather than a discrete failure.
evidence: Shopify daily_7d: Jul7 $0/0 orders, Jul8 $0/0, Jul9 $0/0, Jul10 $0/0 (ShopifyQL, ds=sales)
Klaviyo 7d: total_email_revenue_7d $0 across both active flows (Abandoned Cart Reminder, Warranty Registration Confirmation)

2026-07-12: the zero-streak broke on Jul 11 with exactly 1 order ($34.18) - but this order was fully Meta-pixel-attributed to the 'Save the Shave UGC Bill' campaign (Meta MCP, same $34.18 value, same day), not organic or email. This supports rather than contradicts the underlying hypothesis: the little D2C activity that exists is paid-social-driven, and email/organic contribute effectively nothing right now.

2026-07-13: Jul 12 produced 2 orders ($87.35 total) and Meta reported $29.50 spend -> ~$87 purchase value the same day (ROAS 2.96, Meta MCP) - again the entire day's D2C revenue lines up with paid-social attribution, not organic or email (both Klaviyo flows remain $0 revenue 7d). Third straight instance of this pattern.

2026-07-14: Jul 13 produced 3 orders ($99.95 total), but Meta's Jul 13 standalone spend/ROAS ($26.76 spend, 0.37 ROAS) implies only ~$9.90 of Meta-attributed purchase value - covering roughly 10% of Shopify's $99.95, not the ~100% match seen the prior two days. First partial break in the exact-match pattern; Klaviyo flows continue contributing zero revenue 7d throughout.

2026-07-15: Jul 14 produced $0 / 0 orders (ShopifyQL) - reverted to zero-revenue after three consecutive days of Meta-driven activity (Jul 11-13). Meta's Jul 14 daily ROAS was 0.37, consistent with a weak day that would not drive Shopify conversions. Klaviyo flows continue contributing zero revenue. Pattern holds: D2C activity is entirely dependent on Meta campaign performance day-to-day, with no organic or email baseline.

2026-07-16 (data Jul 15): Jul 15 produced $74.96 / 1 order (ShopifyQL) - a positive Shopify day. Attribution unclear: Meta MTD ROAS on Jul 15 was approximately 0.65 on a ~$25/day spend pace, implying ~$16 Meta-attributed purchase value — does not fully account for the $74.96 order. Browse Abandonment flow remained in DRAFT throughout (Wren firing today to activate). Klaviyo flows still $0 revenue confirmed.

2026-07-17 (data Jul 16): Jul 16 produced $18.99 / 1 order (ShopifyQL). Klaviyo flow report (last 7 days) returned 0 recipients across ALL flows — Welcome Series, Abandoned Cart Reminder, Warranty Registration, Registration all showed zero email sends in past 7 days. This is a significant escalation from prior runs which showed 1-2 sends per week (low volume) — all email activity has now gone to zero. Browse Abandonment remains DRAFT (Wren fired). Total email baseline confirmed at zero: no organic or email floor exists for Shopify.

2026-07-18 (data Jul 17): Jul 17 produced $38.97 / 2 orders / AOV $19.49 (ShopifyQL). vs 7-day avg: -23%. AOV below $34 target threshold. Klaviyo: 0 sends across all live flows for 7d (Welcome Series, Abandoned Cart, Warranty, Registration all confirmed zero recipients — same pattern as yesterday). Browse Abandonment still DRAFT (Wren fired again today). Zero email baseline persists. The $38.97 is positive vs prior day's $18.99 but remains below the 7-day average and below AOV threshold — no sustainable D2C floor visible without email activation or Klaviyo list-health fix.
learnings.action_implication: Klaviyo zero-send streak now confirmed 2+ days across ALL live flows — this is a systemic Klaviyo list or suppression issue, not just Browse Abandonment being in DRAFT. Will should audit the email lists and suppression lists in Klaviyo directly. Browse Abandonment activation (Wren fired daily) is the immediate lever, but zero-send across Welcome, Abandoned Cart, Warranty, and Registration suggests broader list health or deliverability problem. Until fixed, every Shopify order is un-retargetable and cart abandonment recovery is zero. Shopify AOV also tracking below $34 threshold — the $19.49 AOV may indicate lower-priced single-unit purchases without upsell from email sequences.

learnings.subject: Untracked Amazon SKU - FurFix De-Shed Brush
learnings.learning: A 7th Amazon product not in the routine's 6-SKU tracked list ('Skip The Groomer FurFix De-Shed Brush') appeared in the Weekly Amazon Profit report (Jul 6-12) marked 'New', with a net loss (-$23.95) and no revenue/units reported yet - it was launched without being added to the morning routine's tracked-SKU set, echoing the ad-account tracking-hygiene gap from L-2026-07-08-003 but for the organic Amazon catalog rather than ad spend.
evidence: Weekly Amazon Profit Report (Jul 6-12, 2026), Top Products table: 'Skip The Groomer FurFix De-Shed Brush' (B0FQFP2S9F), Gross Revenue '-', Net Profit -$23.95 (tagged 'New'), Units '-'

2026-07-16 (H10 Daily Profit email, Jul 15): FurFix (B0FQFP2S9F) appears in H10 Inventory Insights section with LIF ≤ 0 Days (stockout/no sales) and LTSF ≥ 181 Days — confirming the SKU has 0 units on hand (STRANDED in FBA per Jul 15 SC data) with no active sales velocity. Not yet in roas_bands.json; not spending on ads.
learnings.action_implication: Add B0FQFP2S9F to the Step 4 Helium 10 tracked-SKU list (currently 6 SKUs) and to roas_bands.json if/when it starts spending on ads, so it stops appearing as a surprise line item. Angelo/Will should confirm this is an intentional new-product launch. The STRANDED inventory status (0 units on hand, LIF ≤ 0, LTSF ≥ 181d per Jul 15 SC data) suggests the SKU may have a listing or compliance issue preventing FBA fulfillment — worth checking SC for stranded inventory root cause.

learnings.subject: Slicker Brush (liquidation SKU) receiving ad spend
learnings.learning: Slicker Brush is flagged in roas_bands.json as liquidate=true with tacos_target 'LIQUIDATE' and an explicit note 'Any ad spend > $0 = immediate flag' (no ad funding). Despite this, it shows real ad spend today, the first day per-SKU Amazon Ads data has been available in 6 days - the underlying campaign/targeting was evidently never paused when the SKU was set to liquidate.
evidence: 2026-07-14: Slicker Brush (B0FQ628KCH) spend $1.05, ROAS 0.00 (Supermetrics Query B, date 2026-07-12) - first per-SKU confirmation since the 5-day Supermetrics outage. Prior-day connectors_data.json (2026-07-13) already carried an unverified note referencing 'the Slicker Brush liquidation-SKU ad-spend violation flagged days ago' that could not be confirmed during the outage - this is the first hard data confirming it is still active.

2026-07-15: H10 Daily Keyword Tracker (report date 2026-07-14) shows 'slicker brush for dogs' dropped off rankings entirely — rank absent vs. approximately rank 100 the prior day. Major organic rank collapse for the liquidation SKU. Supermetrics A/B/C failed today so Jul 14 per-SKU ad spend cannot be independently confirmed, but the organic collapse is new evidence of deteriorating listing health. Whether the collapse is driven by the ad spend continuing or by the algorithm deprioritizing a thin listing is unknown without spend data.

2026-07-16 (data Jul 14): Slicker Brush (B0FQ628KCH) shows $0 spend in Supermetrics Query B (date Jul 14) — first data point showing zero spend since the liquidation flag was raised. Query C shows $3.61 in 7d historical spend, confirming spend did occur in the prior 7 days but not on Jul 14 specifically. Additionally H10 Keyword Tracker (Jul 15 report) shows 'doodle brush' at organic rank 144, a 48-spot drop — major rank collapse correlating with the period of confirmed ad spend on a liquidation listing. The Jul 14 $0 spend may indicate the campaign was paused; if confirmed in next run, the spend violation may be resolved while rank damage persists.

2026-07-17 (data Jul 15): Slicker Brush (B0FQ628KCH) shows $1.37 spend in Supermetrics Query B (date Jul 15) — violation ACTIVE AGAIN after Jul 14 $0. The Jul 14 zero-spend was NOT a campaign pause; it was a single-day gap. 7d historical spend (Query C): $5.51. H10 Keyword Tracker (Jul 16 report): 'doodle brush' 144→149 (-5), 'dog slicker brushes for grooming' 129→142 (-13). Organic rank damage continuing alongside active ad spend on a liquidation listing.

2026-07-18 (data Jul 16): Slicker Brush (B0FQ628KCH) shows $1.37 spend in Supermetrics Query B (date Jul 16) — VIOLATION CONTINUES. 7d historical spend (Query C): $6.00. H10 Keyword Tracker (Jul 17 report): 'dog slicker brushes for grooming' rank 261 (-119 from 142) — catastrophic single-day rank collapse. 'doodle brush' rank 151 (-18). 'dog slicker brushes for grooming' has now fallen from approximately rank 100 (Jul 14) to rank 261 — a 161-spot collapse in 3 days. This is the most severe organic rank damage observed for any STG SKU. Ad spend on a liquidation listing continues to correlate with accelerating rank degradation. The campaign has not been paused despite 10+ days of daily VIOLATION flags.
learnings.action_implication: CRITICAL — 10+ consecutive days of Slicker Brush ad spend on a liquidation SKU. Organic rank has collapsed catastrophically: 'dog slicker brushes for grooming' rank ~100→261 in 3 days. @Will must pause the Slicker Brush ad campaign IMMEDIATELY — roas_bands.json is explicit: 'Any ad spend > $0 = immediate flag.' Every additional day of spend accelerates organic rank decay that will take weeks to recover. Until the campaign is confirmed paused for 3+ consecutive days ($0 in Query B for 3 days), this remains an active VIOLATION. Consider whether the liquidation can even succeed if the organic rank continues collapsing — at rank 261, organic discovery is near-zero.

learnings.subject: QuickBooks MTD P&L sync lag
learnings.learning: QuickBooks' MTD revenue/COGS figures have been frozen at the same value for two consecutive daily runs despite Shopify and Amazon (H10) showing real sales activity on the intervening days - the QB connector appears to be lagging real transaction data by at least a day, possibly due to bank-feed or Amazon settlement import delay.
evidence: 2026-07-13 run: MTD period noted as 'Jul 1-12 (connector returned figures identical to yesterday's Jul 1-11 report - likely sync lag)', revenue $3,248.82

2026-07-14 run: MTD period Jul 1-13 still shows revenue $3,248.82 and COGS $1,441.47 - identical to the 2026-07-13 run's Jul 1-12 figures - despite Shopify reporting $87.35 (7/12) + $99.95 (7/13) in sales and H10 reporting $2,210.12 (7/12) + $1,759.05 (7/13) gross Amazon revenue in that window (profit_loss_quickbooks_account, periodStart 2026-07-01, periodEnd 2026-07-13). Operating expenses DID change day over day ($16,872.68 -> $18,810.32), so the lag is isolated to revenue/COGS, not the whole report.

2026-07-15 run: QB MTD revenue $3,224.05 (Jul 1-15) — a slight decrease from the prior $3,248.82 despite two additional days of sales, which may reflect a credit memo, refund, or period-boundary difference rather than new revenue posting. H10 reports $1,537.79 gross on Jul 14 alone, and $1,759.05 on Jul 13, making it evident that QB is capturing less than 2 full days of H10-reported revenue across a 15-day period. This is the 3rd consecutive day of confirmed sync lag — per the original action_implication, this now meets the threshold for escalation as a connector-level bug rather than a timing artifact.

2026-07-16 run: QB MTD net income -$24,532.81 (Jul 1-16). Checking account balance ~$3,166 — significantly below the $25k alert threshold, consistent with QB capturing only a fraction of actual Amazon/Shopify revenue. H10 Jul 15 alone shows $1,496.54 gross. 4th consecutive day of confirmed sync lag. Escalation is overdue — QB revenue figures cannot be used for financial decision-making.

2026-07-17 run: QB MTD revenue $3,207.90 (Jul 1-17). Cash confirmed at $587,217 (Checking $13,166 + MM $574,051). H10 Jul 16: $1,721.89 gross / $864.57 net / 94 units — none of which appears in QB MTD. 5th consecutive day of sync lag. Cash balance figure appears stable because MM balance absorbs daily draws. QB income line is functionally useless for current decision-making.

2026-07-18 run: QB cash $512,334 (Checking $13,283 + MM $499,051). MM dropped ~$75K overnight ($574K → $499K) — likely a large AP payment. Fresh 28d P&L (Jun 20–Jul 18) shows total operating expenses $66,197.41 → weekly burn $16,549/wk. QB MTD revenue still frozen (same figures as prior run despite H10 Jul 17: $1,651.20 gross / $830.46 net / 81 units). 6th consecutive day of sync lag. Runway decreased from 34.3 to 31.0 weeks, reflecting the MM payment rather than new burn. AP total unchanged at $48,504.75 pending confirmation of which vendor received the ~$75K payment.
learnings.action_implication: Do not use QB MTD revenue as a same-day-accurate figure — cross-check against Shopify/H10 native sales data instead. ESCALATION OVERDUE (6th consecutive day): flag to Will and bookkeeper as a connector-level bug requiring investigation of QB's Amazon/Shopify bank-feed sync settings. NEW FLAG: MM balance dropped ~$75K overnight (Jul 17→18: $574K→$499K) — if this was an AP payment, verify which vendor was paid and update AP records; if unexplained, investigate immediately. Most likely causes for sync lag: (1) Amazon settlement import is off or paused in QB, (2) the Shopify-QB sync app is not writing to the revenue account, or (3) QB is operating on a cash-basis with settlement delays longer than expected. Use H10 for revenue, QB for cash and expenses.

learnings.subject: FBA inventory retrieval — Chrome scrape dependency, ASELL no inventory fields
learnings.learning: FBA inventory data (stock levels by SKU) is retrieved via a direct Seller Central browser scrape (Step 5c) rather than through the Supermetrics ASELL data source. The Supermetrics ASELL data source returns sales and session metrics only and does not expose FBA inventory fields in its schema. When Chrome is unavailable (cloud execution environment), Step 5c degrades entirely and inventory_data.json is written with a degraded notice — no inventory data is collected that run.
evidence: 2026-07-15 (v10.8 first run): Chrome browser not available in cloud execution environment; Seller Central scrape (Step 5c) could not execute. Supermetrics ASELL queries (D/E) return sales revenue, sessions, and units fields only — no inventory stock, reserved, or inbound fields present. inventory_data.json written as degraded ({as_of: '2026-07-15', note: 'FBA inventory unavailable — Seller Central scrape failed. @josh', skus: []}).

2026-07-16 run: Chrome still unavailable. inventory_data.json again written as degraded. H10 Daily Profit email (Jul 15) provides partial substitute: Inventory Insights section flags B0FQ4PY573 (Detangling Spray) and B0FQFP2S9F (FurFix) with LIF ≤ 0 Days (stockout risk) and LTSF ≥ 181 Days — these are the highest-priority manual check items.
2026-07-17 run: Chrome still unavailable (3rd consecutive day). inventory_data.json updated with as_of date but retaining Jul 15 SC last-known SKU data. Last-known data is now 2 days stale.

2026-07-18 run: Chrome still unavailable (4th consecutive day). inventory_data.json retains Jul 17 manual Josh pull data (1 day stale — better than prior 2-day stale). Josh manually pulled SC inventory on Jul 17 ~11 AM PT, so current data is 1 day old. Priority manual check items remain: B0FQ4PY573 (Detangling Spray, LIF≤0, listing CLOSED), B0FQFP2S9F (FurFix, STRANDED), B0FQF2VTMJ (Rake Brush, LOW_STOCK ~32 DoS as of Jul 17).
learnings.action_implication: FBA inventory retrieval requires Chrome access to Seller Central — it cannot be satisfied by Supermetrics ASELL. In automated cloud runs without Chrome, inventory_data.json will be written as degraded every run. The Jul 17 manual Josh pull has kept inventory_data.json 1-day fresh — this is the best available workaround. Options: (1) provision Chrome/browser in the cloud environment for automated scrape, (2) Josh continues daily manual SC pull as out-of-band feed to inventory_data.json, (3) evaluate SP-API GetFBAInventory as a direct API alternative that does not require a browser or Supermetrics. PRIORITY items for manual check today: B0FQ4PY573 (Detangling Spray, LIF ≤ 0), B0FQFP2S9F (FurFix, LIF ≤ 0, STRANDED), B0FQF2VTMJ (Rake Brush, ~32 DoS, LOW_STOCK).

learnings.subject: Meta CTR vs. ROAS mismatch — Save the Shave UGC Bill
learnings.learning: Save the Shave UGC Bill has a 6.05% CTR (3× the 1%+ video benchmark) but L30D ROAS 0.64 — the creative hook is stopping the scroll but the post-click conversion chain (landing page, offer clarity, bundle price, checkout friction) is failing. This is a landing page/offer problem, not a creative hook problem.
evidence: 2026-07-16 Blaze 2F audit: CTR 6.05% (stable per prior watchdog data), Frequency 1.53 (below 3.0 fatigue threshold), Engagement Rate Ranking: Above Average (Meta MCP auction data) — creative hook confirmed working
L30D ROAS 0.64 on $313.87 spend → 6 purchases → ~$52 cost per purchase. Meta Conversion Rate Ranking: Average. Diagnosis pattern: CTR ok + CVR low = offer/landing page mismatch

ROAS declined 6+ consecutive days (0.84→0.79→0.75→0.71→0.70→0.65→0.64) despite stable CTR and low frequency — rules out audience exhaustion and creative fatigue as root causes; confirms the problem is post-click

2026-07-18 (data Jul 17): Yesterday account-level spend $27.19, 419 impressions, 24 clicks, CTR 5.73% — CTR remains in 5.7-6.1% range consistently, confirming the hook continues working day-over-day. L30D ROAS now 0.63 (continued decline). No ROAS data available for yesterday specifically (no conversion events attributed on Jul 17). The post-click failure pattern is unbroken: high CTR, sub-1.0 ROAS, no landing page or offer change recorded in the account. A new Shampoo 50OFF UGC campaign was launched Jul 17 (PAUSED) — unclear if this represents a creative strategy shift or a new test vehicle. The core landing page/CVR diagnosis remains unaddressed.
learnings.action_implication: Before launching new creative hooks, audit the Save the Shave Bundle landing page: URL accuracy, price clarity, mobile checkout experience, product-ad alignment. Consider testing Emergency Dematter (single product, lower price point, clearer rescue use case) vs. the bundle to isolate whether offer complexity or price is driving poor CVR. Do not conclude the hook is broken based on ROAS alone — CTR consistently confirms the hook works. The new Shampoo campaign (PAUSED, Jul 17) may be an attempt to address this — verify intent.

learnings.subject: Emergency Dematter — cross-channel performance leader
learnings.learning: Emergency Dematter is the strongest performing STG SKU across both channels. Amazon: ROAS 1.993 (YELLOW, closest to breakeven of any actively-spending SKU), organic rank #3 for 'dematting spray for dogs' (SV 1,829). Meta: two RESCUE-format creative concepts already staged and production-ready. The Save the Shave Bundle (current Meta hero) underperforms (ROAS 0.64) while Dematter shows the clearest cross-channel conversion signal.
evidence: 2026-07-16 Blaze 2F audit: Amazon Dematter ROAS 1.993 (Supermetrics Query B, Jul 14 data) — highest ROAS of any actively-spending SKU in the account

H10 Jul 15: 'dematting spray for dogs' rank 3 (SV 1,829), 'detangler for dogs' rank 14 (+4 improvement), 'dematting comb for dogs' rank 22 (+4 improvement) — three of four tracked Dematter keywords stable or improving
Two Dematter RESCUE creative briefs already staged for Meta: blaze_ten-oclock-mat_2026-07-15 and blaze_she-almost-got-the-shave_2026-07-15 — production-ready pending Will approval

2026-07-17 update: Dematter ROAS fell to RED band (1.578) on Jul 15 — the 'cross-channel leader' designation is still intact for Amazon volume (94 units Jul 16, $1,722 gross) but the paid ROAS decline weakens the claim. Two organic keywords also dropped (detangler for dogs -3, dematting comb for dogs -4). Monitor paid ROAS for recovery — if RED persists 2+ days, reconsider as hero SKU for Meta test until Amazon paid ROAS stabilizes.

2026-07-18 (data Jul 16): Dematter paid ROAS 1.589 (RED band, 2nd consecutive RED day: Jul 15 1.578, Jul 16 1.589). Spend $156.23, attributed sales $248.29 (Supermetrics Query B). TACOS 141.2% (7d spend $1,454.59 / 7d sales $1,029.92) — well above 35% target. Organic keywords: H10 Jul 17 report shows recovery (+2 on two keywords after prior day drops). Dematter remains the highest-revenue SKU by volume (H10 Jul 17: 81 units account-wide, Dematter primary driver). The 2nd consecutive RED paid ROAS day (per the Jul 17 monitoring note) means the Red-persists-2+ threshold has been reached — caution is warranted before committing new Meta budget to Dematter. However the RESCUE creatives remain staged and the organic recovery today suggests listing health is not in structural decline.
learnings.action_implication: Dematter paid ROAS has now been RED for 2 consecutive days (Jul 15 and Jul 16) — the monitoring threshold from the Jul 17 update has been reached. Do NOT commit new Meta budget to Dematter until Amazon paid ROAS recovers to YELLOW or better for 2+ consecutive days. Hold the staged RESCUE creatives (blaze_ten-oclock-mat and blaze_she-almost-got-the-shave) until paid ROAS stabilizes. Dematter remains the strongest volume SKU organically; the paid ROAS issue may reflect attribution lag or competitive dynamics rather than a creative or landing-page failure. Monitor Jul 18 (data Jul 17) Supermetrics data for paid ROAS recovery signal — if GREEN/YELLOW, the Meta test case can be re-activated.
